title: Fast Path vs Full Search — The Two Pillars
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: A two-phase search (fast rejection + full verification) outperforms pure full search by 10-100× when the rejection rate exceeds 90%
content.proof: Fast path (Bloom filter): 2ns, rejects 95% of lookups. Full search: 180ns, handles the remaining 5%. Average time: 0.95 × 2ns + 0.05 × 180ns = 10.9ns. Pure full search: 180ns. Speedup: 16.5×. For higher rejection rates (99%): speedup approaches 90×.
content.benchmark: Two-phase (95% rejection): 10.9ns average. Two-phase (99% rejection): 3.6ns average. Pure full search: 180ns. Pure fast path: 2ns but 0.08% false positives.
content.caveat: Requires tuning: Bloom filter size, number of hash functions, and expected rejection rate must match your workload. Mismatched parameters waste memory or increase false positives.
